threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of 'the Overground' when you hop on at Dalston Junction. That's an instant giveaway.
Caught a night bus near Brixton last week, and a group of teenagers debated the best chicken spots while a stray cat jumped on the seat next to me.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw kids playing football with a makeshift goal made of empty beer crates on the street, I knew I found my home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how the street art in Kreuzberg shifts with the weather? Rain washes away layers, revealing old tags like ghosts from the past.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're local if they can order a burrito without flinching at the price, but look genuinely shocked when their BART train's late again.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Watched a guy try to start a dance battle on the 49 while the bus was stuck in traffic. It turned into a mini talent show—classic SF chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia’s just about the Gaudí sites. They miss the tiny squares where locals actually gather for vermut, laughing in the sun.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll catch them double-checking the metro map at 9 PM, still baffled that some stations close early on weekends.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look puzzled when the bus driver shouts 'Next stop, Manse Road!' like it’s the most normal thing in the world.
Caught a night bus from Brixton last week, and a group sang 'Wonderwall' off-key the whole way. Only in South London do night rides turn into mini concerts.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I spotted a kid selling homemade lemonade by the canal while their grandma watched from a folding chair—that's when it clicked.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how the U-Bahn stations here have ad campaigns featuring local artists? Always something fresh, never the same boring corporate stuff.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they don’t flinch at the BART delays but still know the best taco truck to hit after 10 PM.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy dressed as a banana argue with a pigeon over a seat on the 5-Fulton. Only in SF do you get that kind of morning entertainment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real charm lies in the little plazas filled with locals enjoying vermut on a weekday afternoon.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their struggle with the metro ticket machines—still staring like it's a Rubik's Cube before heading to the counter.
